bare lowercase atom selector

why: quoted selector with a guard on the clause head
bare camelCase atom selector
multi-keyword selector (two keywords, two colons)
indented clause head still matches (leading whitespace allowed)
whitespace inside the braces around the selector atom
generic {Selector, ...} clause binds an uppercase variable — skipped
two-element generic re-dispatch clause — skipped
catch-all clause binds a bare variable — skipped
underscore catch-all clause — skipped
the -spec line is not a clause head (no opening brace after the paren)
a comment mentioning handle_call is not a clause head (not anchored to line start)
an assignment calling handle_call is not a clause head (text precedes handle_call)
handle_cast is not handle_call — never produces a clause row
a clause body line has no selector
an empty line has no selector
drift canary: opening quote without a closing quote still matches — the quotes are independently optional (both impls agree)
drift canary: closing quote without an opening quote still matches — the quotes are independently optional (both impls agree)
drift canary: only one optional closing quote is consumed, so the second quote blocks the trailing comma — no match (both impls agree)
drift canary: a tab indent matches — pins the whitespace set (Rust explicit ' \t\r\v\f' = PCRE non-Unicode \s) against edits to either side
drift canary: a selector with no comma inside the tuple is not a concrete clause — the trailing comma is mandatory (both impls agree)
drift canary: whitespace between handle_call and the paren breaks the anchored prefix — no match (both impls agree)
